6 min read
Just now

--

Artificial intelligence is a big technological breakthrough that got people talking in recent years. It’s a tool that can reduce workload particularly at the office. It allows people to focus on more important tasks. A person’s capability can be improved by artificial intelligence in thereby increasing productivity and yet the same technology presents potential risk of unemployment and unhealthy laziness.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Igor Omilaev on UnsplashAI devices can resemble human beings, this seems out of this world, and it can appear scary depending on perspective. A clear reason for artificial intelligence’s popularity is its ability to simplify difficult intellectual tasks. However, its potential to replace a person in a variety of aspects that include emotionally demanding tasks, can be daunting. AI can present a simple choice before considering its potential harmful consequences, anyone in their correct senses would rather get on a bus than walk for hundreds of kilometres. That’s the matter with working on computers these days.
Here is an explanation to how artificial intelligence works. Artificial intelligence is based on a simple idea that copies natural human intelligence. Instead of the central nervous system it utilises neural networks. Soon after birth a child’s mind carries limited conscious knowledge apart from managing unconscious processes such as breathing, muscle movement, sight and so on. The child begins to slowly capture and store information such as images, sounds, words etc. This information is later retrieved and utilised to speak, make decisions, and develop ideas. Artificial intelligence works in an roughly similar way. To make sure we are on the same page, another word for information is data.
A large computer with lots of memory, that processes information at high speed, known as a server, is loaded with information. Similar to a child who stores information such as words, a data server can be loaded with information that can be later retrieved to complete tasks, make decisions and develop ideas utilising complex code written in a programming language. Python is a popular example of a programming language.
Artificial intelligence can speak human languages, for example English. To get an idea of how this work, think of it this way. To allow AI to speak English a data server can be loaded with a table with numerous words. The table is saved in computer software known as a database, on server. This table can comprise of columns of grouped words with column names such as nouns, verbs, adverbs, adjectives, pronouns, synonyms, definitions etc. This table can have billions of rows with billions of words. The artificial intelligence software can be taught to store additional words from books, websites, PDFs and social media. A fast server can access billions of rows of English language data, quickly to develop a sentence based on instructions given to it through complex python code. A sentence can be constructed by combining words from different columns and utilising basic sentence construction rules encompassed in the code to allow Artificial intelligence to speak. Modern AI can as well convert words to numbers to learn the relationship between the numbers, so that it can mathematically predict the best combination of words based on what it learns in books and the internet. AI depends on big data, in other words a lot of information. This is accompanied by a need for a lot of memory, complex programming, and fast computer systems to quickly access the data and provide answers.
When a person types a sentence the AI system might scan through the words, compare them to words in a database and respond based on programmed conditions. These conditions are known as conditional statements in computer programming. Cleverness, complexness, and nesting of these conditions within each other can result in Artificial Intelligence doing extra ordinary work. Responses can similarly be provided based on mathematical and statistical predictions utilising available information. A lot happens in a few seconds when a person asks a question to get a response. Common questions can be stored to provide answers quicker. A voice can be generated by storing plenty of information about sound waves and phonetics and mapping it to written text. Sound is a wave whose properties such as frequency, wavelength, amplitude, and energy can be quantified and manipulated. These properties can be reverse engineered to produce voices. When utilised correctly specific data about the properties of sound waves and words in question can be manipulated through computer programming to vary electrical current reaching the speakers on a device to synthesise human like voices. Sound waves rely on vibrations. When a current reaching a speaker alters the speaker’s vibrations in a controlled way Artificial intelligence can produce a voice. If you think about it, technology like this takes a lot of work, time, research, people, information, and powerful computer hardware to produce.
This blog post was designed to be as understandable as possible this is an almost impossible task considering the complexity of AI. There are a lot of common terms and explanations that were not mentioned in this post that can be researched as a matter of interest, e.g. concatenation, model training, web scraping, data wrangling, linear regression, tensor flow, datasets, data structures, arrays, machine learning, statistical analysis, etc.
Time shall review whether we should be thankful for the advancement of Artificial Intelligence or not. However, history has shown that people are progressive. They invent and adapt to their own inventions and to changes in their surroundings. A similar occurrence to the introduction of AI in history is the industrial revolution that left workers hopeless after the introduction of machines and steam engines. There was a period of misery before the situation was rescued by; the need for human involvement, emergence of other opportunities that had nothing to do with the machines and agreements between machine owners and the public, that led to formation of workers unions and new professions. Presently, many employees have been dismissed already because of AI advancement.
The introduction of the Google search engine and YouTube provided a lot of answers to people; in need of repairs, to students, to sick people, etcetera. These answers were once solely provided by professionals in the fields of profession. It could have seemed like the end some careers and particular jobs were essentially lost. However, the people adjusted to these technological developments. Another example is WhatsApp. When in was introduced, it was too affordable compared to texts and phone calls. It appeared to me like the downfall of network service providers.
Artificial intelligence is not independent it relies on people to ask questions, define problems, set objectives, and accept and reject its output. This can be a lot of work and it can provide an opportunity for new jobs and methods of doing work.
When it’s all said and done, it might be a smart idea to minimise excessive dependence on AI. A big danger in the utilisation of this technology is its application for dissolute intentions e.g. development of fake news, presentation of false digital evidence, internet security threats, etc. Nevertheless, if AI becomes a threat of this kind, responsible experts and victims might be able to counter it with its AI counterparts.
If you think about it, AI existed in the 1980’s. However it was too undeveloped to do what it can do now. At this time programming code was basic, computer hardware was not as advanced as it is nowadays, and the amount of available data was limited. In the 1980’s a person could play a game of chess against a computer. This was primitive artificial intelligence.
The regulatory authorities and developers of artificial intelligence must have ensured that there is a kill switch for when dangerous situations arise. If artificial intelligence becomes a plague on Earth. They can as well send all the humanoid robots to planet Venus, to build their own Cybertron.
